# OMS — LLM Reference

Multi-agent discussion engine. OMS answers "what should we build and why?" — not "how do we build it."
Skills: /oms (discuss) → /oms-implement (execute) → /oms-train (validate) → /oms-capture (capture failures) → /oms-start (init project)
Full system diagram + change log: `~/.claude/agents/oms-work/overview.md`

## Triggers

Use /oms when:
- Architecture, design, or tech strategy decisions needed
- Task spans 2+ domains (frontend + backend, security + UX, product + eng)
- High-stakes or irreversible changes
- Conflicting approaches exist and you need a structured decision
- Research synthesis needs to become product direction

Skip /oms when:
- Decision is already made — just implement it
- Task is trivial / single-domain / obviously reversible
- You have `action_items[]` from a prior OMS session → use /oms-implement directly

## Invocation modes

```
/oms <task>                  Task mode (default)
/oms read <file|url>         Onboard — team reads material, surfaces questions
/oms discover                Explicit project bootstrap
/oms exec                    C-suite strategic discussion (auto-triggered at milestones)
/oms audit                   Audit — manual full efficiency audit; triggers context-optimizer in full mode
```

## Hierarchy

Every task in the queue traces back through this chain — no exceptions:

```
Milestone          product-direction.ctx.md — named product goal with success criteria
    └── Action Item    Synthesizer output — concrete action advancing that milestone
              └── Task     cleared-queue.md — one-session unit with full OpenSpec
```

- One milestone → many action items (across multiple OMS sessions over time)
- One action item → one task; or two tasks if Elaboration Agent splits (research + impl)
- A task without an action item is not allowed — it has no milestone anchor
- An action item with `feature: "none"` is allowed but rare — signals drift from roadmap

## First-run check (Step 0)

Check for `.claude/agents/router.ctx.md`:
- Not found → run project discovery (read CLAUDE.md, codemap, package.json, tsconfig → generate ctx files)
- Found → skip

Check for `.claude/agents/company-belief.ctx.md`:
- Not found → tell CEO: "Run /oms-start first" → stop
- Found → proceed

Queue state check (always — runs after bootstrap checks):
- Read `[project]/.claude/cleared-queue.md`
- If `cto-stop` or `needs-review` tasks exist → brief CEO before accepting new work:
  `"⚑ N tasks need attention: TASK-NNN (cto-stop), TASK-NNN (needs-review — upstream interface changed)"`
  - `cto-stop` → re-spec inline; new TASK-NNN written; oms-work picks up next run
  - `needs-review` → interface actually changed (oms-work verified); CEO re-specs this task only
  - skip any → proceed to new task
- If no `queued` tasks remain (queue empty or all done/cto-stop/needs-review) AND CEO provides no task →
  auto-trigger exec session (CPO + CTO + CFO + CLO + CRO) to pick ONE milestone and queue its tasks
  → PM gap report injected into CPO's briefing (not an active discussion agent)
  → one exec session = one milestone selected = action_items for that milestone queued
  → repeat next time queue empties
- Otherwise → proceed normally

## Tier activation (Router output)

| Tier | Signals | Max agents | Max rounds |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 Trivial | 1 domain, known pattern, reversible | 1 | 1 |
| 1 Simple | 1–2 domains, needs analysis, reversible | 2 | 2 |
| 2 Compound | 2–3 domains, tradeoffs, partial reversibility | 3 | 2 |
| 3 Complex | 3+ domains OR irreversible OR high uncertainty | 5 | 4 |

Escalation: agents disagree at Tier N after round cap → Tier N+1, resume from Round 2.

## Steps

```
Step 0   Project bootstrap (first run only)
Step 1   Router (Haiku) → task_id, tier, agents, briefings
Step 1.5 Path Diversity (Haiku, Tier 2+ only) → distinct starting frames per agent
Step 2   Round 1 — agents in parallel (blind NGT)
Step 3   Rounds 2+ (Tier 2+ only) — Pre-Facilitator (Haiku, runs every round) → Full Facilitator (Sonnet, fires only if intervention needed)
Step 3.5 CEO Gate (Tier 1+) → Phase 1: classify (Haiku); Phase 2: C-suite blind NGT 1 round if triggered (CPO+CTO+CFO+CLO); if C-suite resolves → inject constraint; if not → CEO Brief + PAUSE; CEO response injected before Step 4
Step 4   Synthesizer (Sonnet; Opus if 5+ agents or livelock)
Step 5   Log to logs/tasks/[task-id].md
Step 6   Trainer (always) — evaluates router + agents at tier scope
Step 7   Context Optimizer (always) — lightweight post-task check; full audit at 10-task threshold, milestone, or /oms audit
Step 8   CEO feedback + scenario capture
Step 8.5 Queue Commit — classify action_items[], write executable tasks to cleared-queue.md
```

## Step 7 — Context Optimizer

Context Optimizer always runs after Trainer. Two modes based on state.

**Always (post-task lightweight check)**:
- Input: `logs/tasks/[task-id].md` + `context-optimizer/metrics.md`
- Check: over-activated agents, wasted rounds, task log size, briefing bloat
- Auto-fix safe ops immediately (facts.json consolidation if >40 entries)
- Increment `task_count_since_last_audit` in `metrics.md`
- If status `clean`: no CEO display — silent
- If `warning` or `action-taken`: append to task log under `## Efficiency Check` only

**Full audit triggers** (run full audit mode instead of lightweight):
- `task_count_since_last_audit` reaches 10
- Router output included `milestone_reached: true`
- CEO ran `/oms audit`

**Full audit**:
- Input: all project ctx files, activated agent personas, last 10 task logs, facts.json, MEMORY.md
- Check: persona bloat, unused ctx files, over-activation patterns, round efficiency, memory bloat
- Auto-fix: facts consolidation, task log archiving, metrics update
- Warn: persona trim needed, unused ctx, Router over-activation pattern, MEMORY consolidation needed
- Output to CEO: 3-5 bullet summary only (not full JSON)
- Reset `task_count_since_last_audit` to 0 in `metrics.md`

**`/oms audit` (on-demand)**:
- Always runs full audit mode regardless of task count
- CEO sees full audit output including recommendations

## Step 8.5 — Queue Commit

Fires after CEO feedback confirms the synthesis. Four sub-steps: Classify → Elaborate → Review → Commit.

---

### Sub-step 1 — Classify

Synthesizer now tags each action_item with `type`, `infra_critical`, and `depends_on` — use these directly.

For each action_item[]:
- `executable` — type is impl or research, scope fits one Claude session, no open decisions → proceed to Elaborate
- `strategic` — requires CEO/CTO decision before work begins → log to `ceo-decisions.ctx.md`, skip

**Chain pre-check** (before Elaborate): if `depends_on` is non-empty, read `chain_type` from the Synthesizer output — do not infer it:
- `chain_type: "value_substitution"` → both tasks proceed to Elaborate; impl gets `Depends:` set
- `chain_type: "direction_selection"` → research task proceeds to Elaborate; impl is held as strategic in `ceo-decisions.ctx.md`

`chain_type` is set by the Synthesizer during the discussion (it knows the context). The Elaboration agent reads it as a field — no inference, no judgment call at this stage.

---

### Sub-step 2 — Elaborate (Task Elaboration Agent)

Agent: `~/.claude/agents/task-elaboration/persona.md` | Model: Sonnet

For each `executable` item, the elaboration agent drafts a complete task block:
- `Spec:` — `The system SHALL [verb] [object] so that [outcome].`
- `Scenarios:` — `GIVEN [pre] WHEN [trigger] THEN [outcome]` pipe-separated
- `Artifacts:` — `path/file — exports: x, y` pipe-separated (impl) or document sections (research)
- `Produces:` — what downstream tasks' `Context:` needs (or `none`)
- `Verify:` — shell commands (or omit)
- `Context:` — files to pre-load; includes `Produces:` from any upstream Depends task
- `Validation:` — derived from activated agents (see chain table below)
- `Depends:` — set if this task depends on another in this commit batch

**Produces → Context wiring**: when task B depends on task A, copy A's `Produces:` value
verbatim into B's `Context:` field. The executor pre-loads it — B starts knowing A's interface.

**Split rule**: research + impl in one task → two tasks with `Depends`. Chain never exceeds 3 steps.

---

### Sub-step 3 — Lightweight Review (by task type)

One reviewer per task. Run in parallel across all drafted tasks.

| Task type | Reviewer | What they check |
|---|---|---|
| Infra-critical / arch change / new service | CTO | Spec is architecturally sound; Artifacts are complete; lock-in risk captured |
| Research / produces findings for product | CPO | Scenarios test the right quality signal; Produces is actionable for downstream |
| Standard engineering | EM | Artifacts scope fits one session; Verify commands exist and are valid |

Reviewer outputs: `APPROVE` or `REWORK — [specific issue]`.
On `REWORK`: elaboration agent re-drafts that task. Surface to CEO if second draft still fails.

---

### Sub-step 4 — Queue Gate + Commit

Each approved task must pass all 8 checks before writing:
- [ ] Spec uses SHALL — one correct interpretation
- [ ] Spec makes no forward reference to unknown research output ("whatever research finds" = instant fail)
- [ ] Every scenario is GIVEN/WHEN/THEN — deterministic pass/fail
- [ ] Artifacts lists every file to produce with exports (or document sections for research)
- [ ] Produces declares downstream contract (or explicitly `none`)
- [ ] All Context files exist and are referenced by path
- [ ] No decision required that would change other queued tasks
- [ ] Scope is completable in one Claude session

Check 2 is the structural direction-selection guard: if the Spec cannot be written without knowing
research output, it is a direction_selection that was misclassified. Surface to CEO now.

If any box fails: surface to CEO for re-spec now. Do not write to queue.

**Derive validation chain:**
| Task type | Chain |
|---|---|
| Research | researcher → cro → cpo |
| Engineering (any) | dev → qa → em |
| CTO / infra-critical | dev → cto |

**Write to `[project]/.claude/cleared-queue.md`** using schema from
`~/.claude/agents/oms-work/task-schema.md`. Append; do not overwrite existing tasks.

**TASK-NNN numbering**: read existing queue, increment highest N by 1. Start at TASK-001 if empty.

**Report to CEO:**
```
Queue Commit: X tasks queued for /oms-work, Y items held (strategic decisions).
Queued: TASK-NNN — title, TASK-NNN — title
Held: [item] — needs [decision] before queueing
```

## Engine Roles

| Role | Persona | Lessons | Memory | Model | Fires |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| router | `~/.claude/agents/router/persona.md` | `router/lessons.md` | `router/MEMORY.md` | Haiku | Step 1 |
| path-diversity | `~/.claude/agents/path-diversity/persona.md` | — | — | Haiku | Step 1.5 (T2+) |
| pre-facilitator | `~/.claude/agents/pre-facilitator/persona.md` | — | — | Haiku | Step 3 inline (T2+) |
| facilitator | `~/.claude/agents/facilitator/persona.md` | `facilitator/lessons.md` | — | Sonnet | Step 3 (T2+) |
| verification | `~/.claude/agents/verification/persona.md` | — | — | Sonnet | On-demand |
| ceo-gate | `~/.claude/agents/ceo-gate/persona.md` | — | `ceo-gate/MEMORY.md` | Haiku | Step 3.5 (T1+) |
| synthesizer | `~/.claude/agents/synthesizer/persona.md` | `synthesizer/lessons.md` | — | Sonnet/Opus | Step 4 |
| trainer | `~/.claude/agents/trainer/persona.md` | `trainer/lessons.md` | `trainer/MEMORY.md` | Sonnet | Step 6 |
| context-optimizer | `~/.claude/agents/context-optimizer/persona.md` | `context-optimizer/lessons.md` | `context-optimizer/MEMORY.md` | Haiku | Step 7 (always) + full audit at milestone |

## Agent roster

**Engine** (always available):
router (Haiku), path-diversity (Haiku, T2+), pre-facilitator (Haiku inline, T2+),
facilitator (Sonnet, T2+), verification (Sonnet, on-demand), synthesizer (Sonnet/Opus), trainer (Sonnet),
context-optimizer (Haiku, Step 7 always + full audit at milestone)

**Engineering** (build/arch/debug/plan/refactor/security/test/perf/ui-ux tasks):
cto, product-manager, engineering-manager, frontend-developer, backend-developer, qa-engineer

**C-suite** (exec tasks + domain scope):
cpo (product/roadmap), clo (legal/compliance), cfo (finance/unit economics)

**Research** (research tasks):
chief-research-officer (lead), human-behavior-researcher, data-intelligence-analyst,
+ project-scoped: content-platform, clinical-safety, language-communication,
  philosophy-ethics, cultural-historical, biological-evolutionary

## Context loading per agent (scoped — never give everyone everything)

| Role | Gets |
|---|---|
| router | all shared context + codemap |
| cto | architecture.md, tech-stack.md, cross-agent-patterns.md |
| product-manager | product-direction.ctx.md + cleared-queue.md summary (queued/done/cto-stop counts per milestone) |
| frontend-developer | tech-stack.md + codemap |
| backend-developer | tech-stack.md, architecture.md + codemap |
| qa-engineer | tech-stack.md + codemap |
| ceo-gate | all discussion round outputs + ceo-mandate.ctx.md (project) or ceo-mandate.md (global default) |
| synthesizer | reads from disk log only + ceo_decision constraint if CEO Gate fired + product-direction.ctx.md (for feature tagging) |
| trainer | validation-criteria.md only |

## OMS scope rule

/oms → synthesis with action_items[]
/oms-implement → takes action_items[], implements, CTO review + QA check
Never merge discuss + implement in one /oms invocation.

## Exec mode auto-triggers

Router fires /oms exec automatically when:
- Milestone in product-direction.ctx.md reached
- Queue empty and CEO runs /oms with no task specified
- Research synthesis requires product direction decision
- C-suite agent raises cross-department concern
- CLO raises high/critical legal risk

## Exec session — always active agents

CPO + CTO + CFO + CLO + CRO activate in every exec session. No exceptions.
- CPO (lead): selects the next milestone; produces action_items; updates product-direction.ctx.md post-exec
- CTO: arch prerequisites and technical risk for the chosen milestone
- CFO: cost of the proposed sprint, unit economics, budget constraints
- CLO: legal/compliance flags before tasks are queued
- CRO: research perspective — what is known, what is unknown, what needs research before building

PM does NOT participate as a discussion agent in exec sessions.
PM gap report (milestone status: complete / in-progress / no coverage) is generated at Step 0
and injected into CPO's briefing as context — not a discussion participant.

One exec session = one milestone selected AND planned. action_items for that milestone are
queued at Step 8.5 in the same session. When queue empties → exec fires → next milestone.

## Task log format

```
logs/tasks/YYYY-MM-DD-short-slug.md
# [task-id]: [CEO intent verbatim]
Date: YYYY-MM-DD  Tier: N  Domain Lead: [role]  Agents: [list]  Rounds: N
```

## Lazy log access

| When | Load |
|---|---|
| Round N | Only Round 1 to Round N-1 sections |
| Synthesis | All round sections (not preamble) |
| Status check | .checkpoint file only |

## Error handling

| Error | Response |
|---|---|
| Invalid agent JSON | Re-run with schema reminder |
| Stage-Gate 2 failure | Re-run failing agent before Round 2 |
| Router cannot route | Ask CEO for more context |
| Stage-Gate 4 failure | Re-run Synthesizer with traceability instruction |
| Hard cap hit | Synthesize best available; escalate if unresolved |

## Related skills

| Skill | When |
|---|---|
| /oms-start | Init new project — run before first /oms |
| /oms-implement | Execute action_items[] from completed synthesis |
| /oms-train | Run training scenarios against agent personas |
| /oms-capture | Capture a real failure as a training scenario |
